title: An investigation into the interaction of closely-spaced starting jets
abstract:
The transient, three-dimensional scavenging ﬂow inside a novel two-stroke engine has been investigated both experimentally in a
scaled water model as well as numerically using a commercial CFD code incorporating an unsteady Reynolds averaged Navier–Stokes
(URANS) formulation. The scavenging ﬂow consists of 16 round jets in close proximity of each other and the cylinder wall, developing
from the top of the combustion chamber down towards the exhaust ports located along the wall at the bottom of t
